body:
EPC Construction Software

Construction ERP Software& App for EPC Companies

EPC projects fail at the handoff — Engineering draws, Procurement orders, Construction builds, but no single system connects all three. SiteFlow gives EPC teams live BOQ tracking, real-time procurement visibility, and site execution control in one platform.

✓
BOQ-linked procurement — every PO tied to scope, not guesswork
✓

✓
Live cost vs budget across all three phases — no month-end surprises
✓
Subcontractor billing with milestone and retention controls built in

The Problem
Why EPC Projects Go Over Budget and Behind Schedule
Every EPC contractor faces these problems. Most accept them as the cost of doing complex projects. They are not inevitable.
Card 1: PO vs BOQ; COLOR: structural steel dot rose
Purchase Orders — vs BOQ Budget
Structural Steel↑ 28% over BOQ
Electrical Conduit↑ 11% over BOQ
Civil FormworkWithin BOQ
POs raised without checking BOQ budget limits
Procurement teams order based on vendor quotes, not BOQ scope. By the time engineering reconciles, commitments are already made and overruns are locked in.
Card 2: Phase silos
📐EngineeringAutoCAD / Excel
≠
📦ProcurementERP / Emails
≠
🏗️ConstructionWhatsApp / Sheets
Three phases, three tools, zero connection
Engineering works in CAD and Excel. Procurement manages POs in a separate ERP. Construction tracks progress on WhatsApp. Management sees a consolidated picture only at month-end — too late to act.
Card 3: Subcontractor billing
Subcontractor Running Bills — Month 7
MEP — Electrical PackageBill: 68%  |  Work: 41%
Civil — Foundation PackageBill: 55%  |  Work: 47%
Steel FabricationBill: 30%  |  Work: 29%
⚠️₹42L paid over verified progress
Subcontractors billed on time, not on work done
Without progress-linked billing, subcontractors raise running bills based on schedule rather than verified installed quantities. Retention tracking is manual, and disputes compound at handover.
Card 4: Labour unverified
Site A — Pune148Register says 174
Site B — Nagpur92Unverified shift
Site C — Nanded61GPS confirmed
Site D — Solapur44No DPR filed
Labour attendance unverified across dispersed sites
EPC projects run across multiple locations simultaneously. Without GPS-based attendance, head office relies on site registers — which are wrong, delayed, or manipulated. Ghost workers and shift discrepancies go undetected until payroll.
Card 5: Full width cost overrun
₹3.8Cr
average cost overrun discovered at month-end — after the damage is done
Month 7 — Overrun detected
M1
M2
M3

M5
M6
Budget overruns discovered only at month-end — when it is already too late
Without live BOQ-linked cost tracking across Engineering, Procurement, and Construction, EPC teams only see the true financial picture after closing the month’s books. By then, materials are ordered, subcontractors are paid, and the overrun is locked in. SiteFlow shows cost position in real time — before commitments become irreversible.
How It Works
How SiteFlow Works for EPC Companies
From BOQ and drawings to procurement approvals to live site execution — three phases, one connected system.
COLOR: SVG connector rose 01: Left — COLOR: pin rose
📄 Engineering Phase
01
Load Your BOQ, Drawings, and Project Scope
Your onboarding manager sets up the full BOQ structure, item-level budgets, rate library, drawing register, and vendor master before go-live. Engineering data becomes the single source of truth that drives every downstream decision — procurement, billing, and cost control all trace back to the BOQ.
BOQ SetupBudget AllocationDrawing RegisterRate LibraryVendor Master
02: Right — amber pin kept
📦 Procurement Phase
02
Control Every PO, Approval, and Material Delivery
Every material request and purchase order is raised against a specific BOQ line item. Multi-level approval chains check budget availability before any commitment is made. GRNs match against POs at delivery — so bills are only approved for what actually arrived on site, verified by quantity and condition.
BOQ-Linked POsApproval WorkflowsGRN MatchingVendor BillsBudget Checks
03: Left — green pin kept
🏗 Construction Phase
03
Track Every Site, Worker, and Rupee in Real Time
Site engineers update progress, material consumption, and labour attendance from mobile — no registers, no delays. GPS and face recognition verify workers across all locations simultaneously. Subcontractor running bills generate from approved quantities. P&L updates live as work happens — so overruns are visible days before they become irreversible.
GPS AttendanceMulti-Site DPRProgress TrackingSubcon BillingLive P&L
Core Features
Everything an EPC Team Needs to Stay in Control
From procurement approval to progress billing to site attendance — every function built for EPC project complexity.
ROW 1: BOQ Procurement
Procurement Management

Control Every Purchase Order Against Your BOQ Budget

Every material request in SiteFlow traces back to a BOQ line item and a confirmed budget position. Procurement teams cannot raise a PO that exceeds the approved scope — multi-level approval chains block overspend before it becomes a commitment. GRN matching at delivery ensures bills are only approved for what physically arrived on site.
✓
BOQ-linked POs — every order tied to a scope item and budget limit
✓
Multi-level approvals — Director, PM, and Accounts can each hold a gate
✓
GRN matching — bills approved only against verified site deliveries
✓
Live committed vs available budget — visible before every new PO
See procurement control in action →

Multi-Site Execution

Track Every Site, Worker, and Progress Update in Real Time

EPC projects run across multiple locations simultaneously. SiteFlow gives head office a live view of every site — labour counts verified by GPS and face recognition, daily progress reports submitted from mobile, and cost positions updated as work happens. No waiting for end-of-day calls or WhatsApp updates.
✓
GPS and face recognition attendance — verified across all sites from one dashboard
✓
DPR from mobile — site engineers submit progress in one tap, office gets it instantly
✓
Geo-tagged site photos — timestamped proof attached to every daily report
✓
Live cost vs budget per site — catch overruns before they compound
See multi-site tracking in action →
ROW 3: Subcontractor billing
Subcontractor Management

Bill Subcontractors on Work Done, Not Work Promised

SiteFlow generates subcontractor running bills directly from verified installed quantities and approved progress milestones. Retention percentages are applied automatically. Bills only reach accounts when site verification is complete — so EPC companies never pay for work that has not been confirmed on site.
✓
Progress-linked billing — running bills generated from verified quantities, not schedules
✓
Automatic retention deduction — percentage held and tracked without manual ledgers
✓
Digital work orders — scope, rate, and milestones agreed before work begins
✓
Subcon P&L per package — see profitability on each subcontracted scope
See subcontractor billing in action →

FAQ
FAQ
Questions EPC Companies Ask About SiteFlow
Everything you need to know before switching to SiteFlow for your EPC projects.

SiteFlow uses the BOQ as the connecting layer across all three phases. In Engineering, the BOQ is loaded with quantities, unit rates, and budget allocations. In Procurement, every material request and purchase order is raised against a specific BOQ item — so budget exposure is visible before any commitment is made. In Construction, site engineers update progress against the same BOQ items from mobile. This means cost position, billing readiness, and budget vs actual all update in real time as work progresses — without separate reconciliation between departments.

Yes. SiteFlow is designed for multi-site EPC operations. Head office gets a consolidated view of all active sites — labour counts verified by GPS attendance, daily progress reports submitted by site engineers from mobile, and cost positions updated as work happens. Each site has its own BOQ, budget, and team structure, but leadership can compare performance across sites, track cost exposure at a portfolio level, and receive alerts when any site deviates from plan — all from a single dashboard.

Every purchase order in SiteFlow is raised against a BOQ line item and a confirmed budget position. Before a PO is submitted for approval, the system checks whether the committed amount plus the new PO would exceed the allocated BOQ budget. If it does, the PO is flagged and requires a higher approval level before it can proceed. Multi-level approval chains — from Site Engineer to Project Manager to Director — can each hold a gate. This means overspend is caught before it becomes a committed liability, not after.

SiteFlow generates subcontractor running bills directly from verified installed quantities, not from time or schedule. Before a bill reaches accounts, site engineers confirm the progress milestone on mobile and the system cross-checks against the digital work order. Retention percentages are applied automatically on every bill — no manual ledger maintenance. Final retention releases are tracked and triggered when verified completion conditions are met. This eliminates disputes at handover and ensures EPC companies never pay ahead of confirmed work.

SiteFlow integrates natively with Tally and Zoho Books, eliminating double entry between site operations and accounts. Vendor bills approved in SiteFlow sync to your accounting system automatically. Labour payroll calculated from verified GPS attendance exports directly for salary processing. EPC companies using SiteFlow typically stop maintaining parallel Excel sheets within the first month of go-live because all financial data flows from site activity directly into their accounting system without re-keying.

Most EPC teams are operational within 3 to 7 days. SiteFlow provides a dedicated onboarding manager at no extra cost who sets up your project structure, BOQ, rate library, vendor master, and user access before go-live. Site engineers typically need less than an hour of training to start submitting DPRs and attendance from mobile. Unlike complex ERP implementations that take months, SiteFlow is designed to match how construction teams actually work — so adoption happens on site, not in a training room.

Yes. SiteFlow is actively used by EPC and construction teams across India and the Gulf — including projects in the UAE, Qatar, Oman, and Saudi Arabia. The platform supports multi-currency projects, mixed Indian and expatriate workforces, and cross-border procurement workflows. GPS attendance works across all geographies. EPC companies managing projects in both India and the Middle East use SiteFlow to maintain consistent reporting, cost tracking, and procurement controls regardless of project location.

Generic ERPs are built for enterprise administration — finance, HR, inventory — adapted for construction as one of many verticals. SiteFlow is built exclusively for construction execution. Every module — BOQ management, procurement, labour attendance, subcontractor billing, progress tracking, DPR — is designed around how EPC projects actually run on site. This means site engineers adopt it without training overhead, field data flows directly into cost views without consolidation, and budget overruns are caught at the procurement stage rather than discovered at month-end close.
